meta.name: ISI Insomnia Severity Index
meta.description: The ISI is a 7-item self-report measure that assesses the nature, severity, and impact of insomnia.
meta.author: Morin CM (1993)
meta.reference: Bastien CH, Vallieres A, Morin CM. Validation of the Insomnia Severity Index as an outcome measure for insomnia research. Sleep Med. 2001;2(4):297-307.
meta.instruction: Please rate the current (i.e., last 2 weeks) severity of your insomnia problem(s).

questions.text: How satisfied/dissatisfied are you with your current sleep pattern
To what extent do you consider your sleep problem to interfere with your daily functioning (e.g., daytime fatigue, ability to function at work/daily chores, concentration, memory, mood)
How noticeable to others do you think your sleep problem is in terms of impairing the quality of your life
How worried/distressed are you about your current sleep problem

ranges.label: No Clinically Significant Insomnia
ranges.description: Your insomnia severity is very low. This indicates that your sleep quality and patterns are in good condition, allowing you to get adequate rest.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good sleep habits
Keep a regular sleep-wake schedule
Continue maintaining your good sleep habits
Keep a consistent sleep schedule
Avoid screen time and stimulating activities before bed

ranges.label: Subthreshold Insomnia
ranges.description: You have mild insomnia that may slightly affect daily life. Mild sleep difficulties are quite common. Adjusting your sleep habits and environment can often bring improvement.
suggestions: Improve sleep hygiene habits
Avoid screens before bedtime
Reduce caffeine and alcohol intake
Establish fixed bedtime and wake-up times
Reduce screen use one hour before bed

ranges.label: Clinical Insomnia (Moderate)
ranges.description: You have moderate insomnia that likely affects daily functioning. At this level, sleep problems may cause daytime fatigue and affect your concentration and emotional stability.
suggestions: Consult a sleep medicine professional
Consider cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I)
Maintain a regular sleep schedule
Consider consulting a doctor to rule out possible sleep disorders
Try Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I)

ranges.label: Clinical Insomnia (Severe)
ranges.description: You have severe insomnia significantly affecting daily functioning. Your sleep difficulties are significant and may be considerably affecting your daytime functioning and overall health. Consulting a sleep specialist is recommended.
suggestions: Seek medical evaluation from a sleep specialist promptly
Pharmacotherapy combined with psychotherapy may be needed
Consult a sleep specialist for a comprehensive evaluation
A sleep study may be needed
Follow the professionally recommended sleep management plan
